SmilesDrawer
Home Playground Getting Started Examples
Home Playground Getting Started Examples

Draw molecules from SMILES

No server, no WASM, no setup.

~200 KB Pure JavaScript 15K+ weekly downloads
Press Enter or just type

Three lines. That's it.

Works everywhere. No build step required.

index.html 
<script src="https://unpkg.com/smiles-drawer/dist/smiles-drawer.min.js"></script>
<svg data-smiles="c1ccccc1"></svg>
<script>SmiDrawer.apply();</script>
Get started Try the playground

How it compares

The only plug-and-play chemistry renderer for the web.

SmilesDrawer RDKit.js OpenChemLib
Bundle size ~200 KB ~11 MB ~2 MB
Technology Pure JS C++ / WASM Java / GWT
Setup 3 lines Async WASM init Moderate
NPM weekly downloads ~15K ~17K ~11K
SVG output ✓ ✓ ✓
Canvas output ✓ — ✓
Dark/light themes ✓ — —

No binary loading, no async init, instant rendering.

Built for the web

Everything you need to render molecules in the browser.

SVG & Canvas output

Render to SVG for crisp vectors or Canvas for raster output. Both work with the same API.

Dark & light themes

Built-in theme support with fully customizable atom and bond colors for both modes.

Reaction SMILES

Render chemical reactions from reaction SMILES notation with reactants, reagents, and products.

Gaussian / heatmap mode

Overlay property heatmaps on molecular structures — unique to SmilesDrawer.

Molecular properties

Extract molecular formula, ring count, and other computed properties from parsed structures.

Fully customizable

Control bond length, thickness, spacing, fonts, padding, stereochemistry display, and more.

Molecule Showcase

Click any molecule to try it in the editor above.

Aspirin
CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O
Caffeine
Cn1c(=O)c2c(ncn2C)n(c1=O)C
Penicillin G
CC1([C@@H](N2[C@H](S1)[C@@H](C2=O)NC(=O)Cc3ccccc3)C(=O)O)C
Cholesterol
C[C@H](CCCC(C)C)[C@H]1CC[C@@H]2[C@@]1(CC[C@H]3[C@H]2CC=C4[C@@]3(CC[C@@H](C4)O)C)C
Nicotine
CN1CCC[C@H]1c2cccnc2
Glucose
OC[C@H]1OC(O)[C@H](O)[C@@H](O)[C@@H]1O
Dopamine
NCCc1ccc(O)c(O)c1
Ibuprofen
CC(C)Cc1ccc(cc1)C(C)C(=O)O
Benzene
c1ccccc1
Naphthalene
c1ccc2ccccc2c1
Morphine
CN1CC[C@]23c4c5ccc(O)c4O[C@H]2[C@@H](O)C=C[C@H]3[C@@H]1C5
Testosterone
C[C@]12CC[C@H]3[C@@H](CCC4=CC(=O)CC[C@@]34C)[C@@H]1CC[C@@H]2O

Ready to draw molecules?
